question_html: Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex}.
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex} .
explanation: Let, {tex}I=\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex} Put, t = sin x dt = cos x dx {tex}I=\int \frac{d t}{(1+t)(2+t)}{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)}=\frac{A}{1+t}+\frac{B}{2+t}{/tex} .... (1) A(2 + t) + B(1 + t) = 1 Now put t + 1 = 0 Therefore, t = -1 A(2 - 1) + B(0) = 1 A = 1 Now put t + 2 = 0 Therefore, t = -2 A(0) + B(-2 + 1) = 1 B = -1 Now From equation (1) we get {tex}\frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)}=\frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{-1}{2+t}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)} d t=\int \frac{1}{1+t} d t-\int \frac{1}{2+t} d t{/tex} {tex}=\log |1+t|-\log |t+2|+c{/tex} {tex}=\log \left|\frac{t+1}{t+2}\right|+c{/tex} So, {tex}I=\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\log \left|\frac{\sin x+1}{\sin x+2}\right|+c{/tex}
solution: Let, {tex}I=\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex} Put, t = sin x dt = cos x dx {tex}I=\int \frac{d t}{(1+t)(2+t)}{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)}=\frac{A}{1+t}+\frac{B}{2+t}{/tex} .... (1) A(2 + t) + B(1 + t) = 1 Now put t + 1 = 0 Therefore, t = -1 A(2 - 1) + B(0) = 1 A = 1 Now put t + 2 = 0 Therefore, t = -2 A(0) + B(-2 + 1) = 1 B = -1 Now From equation (1) we get {tex}\frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)}=\frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{-1}{2+t}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)} d t=\int \frac{1}{1+t} d t-\int \frac{1}{2+t} d t{/tex} {tex}=\log |1+t|-\log |t+2|+c{/tex} {tex}=\log \left|\frac{t+1}{t+2}\right|+c{/tex} So, {tex}I=\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\log \left|\frac{\sin x+1}{\sin x+2}\right|+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let, {tex}I=\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex}
Put, t = sin x
dt = cos x dx
{tex}I=\int \frac{d t}{(1+t)(2+t)}{/tex}
Using partial fractions,
 {tex}\frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)}=\frac{A}{1+t}+\frac{B}{2+t}{/tex} .... (1)
A(2 + t) + B(1 + t) = 1
Now put t + 1 = 0
Therefore, t = -1
A(2 - 1) + B(0) = 1
A = 1
Now put t + 2 = 0
Therefore, t = -2
A(0) + B(-2 + 1) = 1
B = -1
Now From equation (1) we get
{tex}\frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)}=\frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{-1}{2+t}{/tex}
{tex}\int \frac{1}{(1+t)(2+t)} d t=\int \frac{1}{1+t} d t-\int \frac{1}{2+t} d t{/tex}
{tex}=\log |1+t|-\log |t+2|+c{/tex}
{tex}=\log \left|\frac{t+1}{t+2}\right|+c{/tex}
So,
{tex}I=\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex}{tex}=\log \left|\frac{\sin x+1}{\sin x+2}\right|+c{/tex}

question_html: Integrate the function: {tex}\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}{/tex}
question_text: Integrate the function: {tex}\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\left( {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x}}} \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} Adding and subtracting sin x in the numerator, {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x + \sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {\cos x - \sin x} \right) + \left( {\sin x + \cos x} \right)}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {1 + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx-\int {\frac{{ - \sin x - \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} } } \right]dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \log \left| {\cos x - \sin x} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}[\because \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)} dx=log|f(x)|+c]{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\left( {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x}}} \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} Adding and subtracting sin x in the numerator, {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x + \sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {\cos x - \sin x} \right) + \left( {\sin x + \cos x} \right)}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {1 + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx-\int {\frac{{ - \sin x - \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} } } \right]dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \log \left| {\cos x - \sin x} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}[\because \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)} dx=log|f(x)|+c]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\left( {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x}}} \right)}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

Adding and subtracting sin x in the numerator,

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x + \sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {\cos x - \sin x} \right) + \left( {\sin x + \cos x} \right)}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {1 + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx-\int {\frac{{ - \sin x - \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} } } \right]dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \log \left| {\cos x - \sin x} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}[\because \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)} dx=log|f(x)|+c]{/tex}

x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(ii) On adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get , {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( x + \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex} we know that, {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 a } f ( x ) d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { a }f(x) dx \ , \ if \ \ f(2a - x) = f(x){/tex} Here, {tex} a^2 cos^2( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ){/tex} {tex}= a^2 cos^2 x + b^2 sin^2x{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex} On dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}cos^2x{/tex} we get , {tex} 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } \tan ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} Put {tex}tan x = t \implies sec^2 x dx = dt{/tex} Lower limit when x = 0, then {tex}t = tan 0 = 0{/tex} Upper limit when x = {tex} \frac { \pi } { 2 },{/tex} then {tex} t =\tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } = \infty{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } t ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + ( b t ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \frac { \pi } { b ^ { 2 } } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { \left( \frac { a } { b } \right) ^ { 2 } + t ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { b t } { a } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty - \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty = \tan ^ { - solution_html:
According to the question , {tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(ii)
On adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get ,
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( x + \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex}
we know that,{tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 a } f ( x ) d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { a }f(x) dx \ , \ if \ \ f(2a - x) = f(x){/tex}
Here, {tex} a^2 cos^2( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ){/tex}
{tex}= a^2 cos^2 x + b^2 sin^2x{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex}
On dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}cos^2x{/tex} we get ,
{tex} 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } \tan ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}tan x = t \implies   sec^2 x dx = dt{/tex}
Lower limit when x = 0, then {tex}t = tan 0 = 0{/tex}
Upper limit when x = {tex} \frac { \pi } { 2 },{/tex}then {tex} t =\tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } = \infty{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } t ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + ( b t ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex} = \frac { \pi } { b ^ { 2 } } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { \left( \frac { a } { b } \right) ^ { 2 } + t ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { b t } { a } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty - \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 \right]{/tex}{tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty = \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) = \frac { \pi } { 2 } } \\ { \text { and } \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 = \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan 0 ^ { \circ } \right) = 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \therefore I = \frac { \pi ^ { 2 } } { 2 a b }{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{1}^{4}[|x-1|+|x-2|+|x-3|] d x{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{1}^{4}[|x-1|+|x-2|+|x-3|] d x{/tex}
explanation: Given: {tex}\int_{1}^{4}[|x-1|+|x-2|+|x-3|] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-1|+|\mathrm{x}-2|+|\mathrm{x}-3|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-1|] \mathrm{dx}+\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-2|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-3|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} Let I = I 1 + I 2 + I 3 First solve for I 1 : {tex}I_{1}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-1|] d x{/tex} As we can see that {tex}(x-1) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\int_{1}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-1) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-4-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+1\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[8-4-\frac{1}{2}+1\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\left[5-\frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{9}{2}{/tex} Now solve for I 2 : {tex}I_{2}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-2|] d x{/tex} As we can see that {tex}(x-2) \leq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 2{/tex} and {tex}(x-2) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}2 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} as, {tex}\left\{\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{c} f(x) d x+\int_{c}^{b} f(x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\int_{1}^{2}-(\mathrm{x}-2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{2}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-2 \mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{2}+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-2 \mathrm{x}\right]_{2}^{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[\frac{(2)^{2}}{2}-2(2)-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+2(1)\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-2(4)-\frac{(2)^{2}}{2}+2(2)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[2-4-\frac{1}{2}+2\right]+[8-8-2+4]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\left[\frac{1}{2}+2\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} Now solve for I 3 : {tex}I_{3}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-3|] d x{/tex} As we can see that {tex}(x-3) \leq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 3{/tex} and {tex}(x-3) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}3 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} as, {tex}\left\{\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{c} f(x) d x+\int_{c}^{b} f(x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=\int_{1}^{3}-(\mathrm{x}-3) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{3}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-3) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-3 \mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{3}+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-3 \mathrm{x}\right]_{3}^{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{(3)^{2}}{2}-3(3)-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+3(1)\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-3(4)-\frac{(3)^{2}}{2}+3(3)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{9}{2}-9-\frac{1}{2}+3\right]+\left[8-12-\frac{9}{2}+9\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{3}=\left[2+\frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{3}=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} as I = I 1 + I 2 + I 3 {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{9}{2}+\frac{5}{2}+\frac{5}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{19}{2}{/tex}

\mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-1|] \mathrm{dx}+\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-2|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-3|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} Let I = I 1 + I 2 + I 3 First solve for \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-4-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+1\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[8-4-\frac{1}{2}+1\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\left[5-\frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex} \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}2 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} as, {tex}\left\{\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{c} f(x) d x+\int_{c}^{b} f(x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\int_{1}^{2}-(\mathrm{x}-2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{2}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-2 \mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{2}+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-2 \mathrm{x}\right]_{2}^{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[\frac{(2)^{2}}{2}-2(2)-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+2(1)\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-2(4)-\frac{(2)^{2}}{2}+2(2)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[2-4-\frac{1}{2}+2\right]+[8-8-2+4]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\left[\frac{1}{2}+2\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} Now solve for I 3 : {tex}I_{3}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-3|] d x{/tex} As we can see that {tex}(x-3) \leq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 3{/tex} and {tex}(x-3) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}3 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} as, {tex}\left\{\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{c} f(x) d x+\int_{c}^{b} f(x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=\int_{1}^{3}-(\mathrm{x}-3) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{3}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-3) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-3 \mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{3}+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-3 \mathrm{x}\right]_{3}^{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{(3)^{2}}{2}-3(3)-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+3(1)\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-3(4)-\frac{(3)^{2}}{2}+3(3)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{9}{2}-9-\frac{1}{2}+3\right]+\left[8-12-\frac{9}{2}+9\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{3}=\left[2+\frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{3}=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} as I = I 1 + I 2 + I 3 {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{9}{2}+\frac{5}{2}+\frac{5}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{19}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int_{1}^{4}[|x-1|+|x-2|+|x-3|] d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-1|+|\mathrm{x}-2|+|\mathrm{x}-3|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-1|] \mathrm{dx}+\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-2|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{1}^{4}[|\mathrm{x}-3|] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
Let I = I1 + I2 + I3 
First solve for I1:
{tex}I_{1}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-1|] d x{/tex} 
As we can see that {tex}(x-1) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 4{/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\int_{1}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-1) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-4-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+1\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\left[8-4-\frac{1}{2}+1\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\left[5-\frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{9}{2}{/tex} 
Now solve for I2:
{tex}I_{2}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-2|] d x{/tex} 
As we can see that {tex}(x-2) \leq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 2{/tex} and {tex}(x-2) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}2 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} 
as, {tex}\left\{\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{c} f(x) d x+\int_{c}^{b} f(x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\int_{1}^{2}-(\mathrm{x}-2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{2}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-2 \mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{2}+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-2 \mathrm{x}\right]_{2}^{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[\frac{(2)^{2}}{2}-2(2)-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+2(1)\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-2(4)-\frac{(2)^{2}}{2}+2(2)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=-\left[2-4-\frac{1}{2}+2\right]+[8-8-2+4]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\left[\frac{1}{2}+2\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{2}=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} 
Now solve for I3:
{tex}I_{3}=\int_{1}^{4}[|x-3|] d x{/tex} 
As we can see that {tex}(x-3) \leq 0{/tex} when {tex}1 \leq x \leq 3{/tex} and {tex}(x-3) \geq 0{/tex} when {tex}3 \leq x \leq 4{/tex} 
as, {tex}\left\{\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{c} f(x) d x+\int_{c}^{b} f(x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=\int_{1}^{3}-(\mathrm{x}-3) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}+\int_{3}^{4}(\mathrm{x}-3) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-3 \mathrm{x}\right]_{1}^{3}+\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}-3 \mathrm{x}\right]_{3}^{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{(3)^{2}}{2}-3(3)-\frac{(1)^{2}}{2}+3(1)\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{(4)^{2}}{2}-3(4)-\frac{(3)^{2}}{2}+3(3)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{3}=-\left[\frac{9}{2}-9-\frac{1}{2}+3\right]+\left[8-12-\frac{9}{2}+9\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{3}=\left[2+\frac{1}{2}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{3}=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} 
as I = I1 + I2 + I3 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{9}{2}+\frac{5}{2}+\frac{5}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{19}{2}{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x \tan x } { \sec x \cdot \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex}
explanation: According to the question , {tex}I =\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x \tan x } { \sec x \cdot \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex} ........(i) we know that , {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x{/tex} we get {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) \tan ( \pi - x ) } { \sec ( \pi - x ) \text{cosec} ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) ( - \tan x ) } { - \sec x \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) \tan x } { \sec x \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex} ......(ii) On adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get, {tex}2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi \tan x } { \sec x \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x ( \cos x ) } { ( \cos x ) } d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \sin ^ { 2 } x d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \frac { 1 - \cos 2 x } { 2 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 4 } \left[ x - \frac { \sin 2 x } { 2 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { 4 } \left[ \pi - \frac { \sin 2 \pi } { 2 } - 0 + \frac { \sin ( 0 ) } { 2 } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { 4 } [ \pi - 0 ] {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi ^ { 2 } } { 4 }{/tex}

we know that ,  {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x{/tex}
we get
{tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) \tan ( \pi - x ) } { \sec ( \pi - x ) \text{cosec} ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) ( - \tan x ) } { - \sec x \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) \tan x } { \sec x \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex}......(ii)
On adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get,
{tex}2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi \tan x } { \sec x \text{cosec} x } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x ( \cos x ) } { ( \cos x ) } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \sin ^ { 2 } x d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \frac { 1 - \cos 2 x } { 2 } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \pi } { 4 } \left[ x - \frac { \sin 2 x } { 2 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { 4 } \left[ \pi - \frac { \sin 2 \pi } { 2 } - 0 + \frac { \sin ( 0 ) } { 2 } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { 4 } [ \pi - 0 ] {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi ^ { 2 } } { 4 }{/tex}

question_html: Integrate the function {tex}\int_1^4 {\left[ {\left| {x - 1} \right| + \left| {x - 2} \right| + \left| {x - 3} \right|} \right]} dx{/tex}
question_text: Integrate the function {tex}\int_1^4 {\left[ {\left| {x - 1} \right| + \left| {x - 2} \right| + \left| {x - 3} \right|} \right]} dx{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I = \int_1^4 {\left[ {\left| {x - 1} \right| + \left| {x - 2} \right| + \left| {x - 3} \right|} \right]} dx{/tex}

{tex} \int_1^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx = \int_1^4 {(x -1 )} } dx{/tex}

{tex} =[\frac{x^2}{2}-x]_{1}^4=(\frac{16}{2}-4)-(\frac{1}{2}-1){/tex}

{tex}=4+\frac{1}{2}=\frac{9}{2}{/tex}

{tex} \int_1^4 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx = \int_1^2 { - (x - 2)dx + \int_2^4 {(x - 2)dx = \frac{5}{2}} } }{/tex}

{tex}=-[\frac{ x^2}{2}-2x]_1^2+[\frac{x^2}{2}-2x]_2^4{/tex}

{tex}=-[(\frac{4}{2}-4)-(\frac{1}{2}-2)]+[(\frac{16}{2}-8)-(\frac{4}{2}-4)]=\frac{1}{2}+2=\frac{5}2 {/tex}

{tex}\int_1^4 {(x - 3)dx = \int_1^3 { - (x - 3)dx + \int_3^4 {(x - 3)dx = \frac{5}{2}} } } {/tex}

{tex}-[\frac{x^2}{2}-3x]_1^3+[\frac {x^2}{2}-3x]_3^4{/tex}

{tex}=-[(\frac{9}{2}-9)-(\frac{1}{2}-3)]+[(\frac{16}{2}-12)-(\frac{9}{2}-9){/tex}

{tex}=2+\frac{1}{2}=\frac{5}2{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{I} = \frac{9}{2} + \frac{5}{2} + \frac{5}{2} = \frac{{19}}{2}{/tex}

question_text: Integrate the function {tex} \frac{{x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex} Let Linear = A {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{/tex} (Quadratic) + B {tex}\Rightarrow x + 2 = A\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) + B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = A(2x + 2) + B …(ii) {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = 2Ax + 2A + B Comparing coefficients of x, 2A = 1 {tex} \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} Comparing constants, 2A + B = 2 On solving, we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},{/tex} B = 1 Putting the values of A and B in eq. (ii), {tex}x + 2 = \frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1{/tex} Putting this value of x+2 in eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx + \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}{I_1} + {I_2}{/tex} …(iii) Now {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}} dx{/tex} Putting x 2 + 2x + 3 = t {tex} \Rightarrow 2x + 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} (2x + 2)dx = dt {tex}\therefore {I_1} = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \int {{t^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}dt} } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} = 2\sqrt t {/tex} {tex} = 2\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} {/tex} …(iv) Again {tex}{I_2} = \int {\frac{1}{\sqrt{{x^2} + 2x + 3}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 1 + 2} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right|{/tex} …(v) Putting values of I 1 and I 2 in eq. (iii), {tex}I = \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} + \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right| + c{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex} Let Linear = A {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{/tex} (Quadratic) + B {tex}\Rightarrow x + 2 = A\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) + B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = A(2x + 2) + B …(ii) {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = 2Ax + 2A + B Comparing coefficients of x, 2A = 1 {tex} \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} Comparing constants, 2A + B = 2 On solving, we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},{/tex} B = 1 Putting the values of A and B in eq. (ii), {tex}x + 2 = \frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1{/tex} Putting this value of x+2 in eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx + \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}{I_1} + {I_2}{/tex} …(iii) Now {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}} dx{/tex} Putting x 2 + 2x + 3 = t {tex} \Rightarrow 2x + 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} (2x + 2)dx = dt {tex}\therefore {I_1} = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \int {{t^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}dt} } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} = 2\sqrt t {/tex} {tex} = 2\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} {/tex} …(iv) Again {tex}{I_2} = \int {\frac{1}{\sqrt{{x^2} + 2x + 3}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 1 + 2} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right|{/tex} …(v) Putting values of I 1 and I 2 in eq. (iii), {tex}I = \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} + \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex}

Let Linear = A {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{/tex} (Quadratic) + B

{tex}\Rightarrow x + 2 = A\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) + B{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = A(2x + 2) + B …(ii)

{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = 2Ax + 2A + B

Comparing coefficients of x, 2A = 1 {tex} \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

Comparing constants, 2A + B = 2

On solving, we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},{/tex} B = 1

Putting the values of A and B in eq. (ii),

{tex}x + 2 = \frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1{/tex}

Putting this value of x+2 in eq. (i),

{tex}I = \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex}

{tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx + \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} } {/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}{I_1} + {I_2}{/tex} …(iii)

Now {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}} dx{/tex}

Putting x2 + 2x + 3 = t

{tex} \Rightarrow 2x + 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} (2x + 2)dx = dt

{tex}\therefore {I_1} = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \int {{t^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}dt} } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} = 2\sqrt t {/tex}

{tex} = 2\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} {/tex} …(iv)

Again {tex}{I_2} = \int {\frac{1}{\sqrt{{x^2} + 2x + 3}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 1 + 2} }}} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} } \right|{/tex}

{tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right|{/tex}…(v)

Putting values of I1 and I2 in eq. (iii),

{tex}I = \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} + \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right| + c{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\cos ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\cos ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex}
explanation: Let the given integral be I = {tex}\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\cos ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \ I=\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\left(1-\sin ^{2} x\right)-4 \sin x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 1=\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-1+\sin ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex} Substitute sin x = t => cos x dx = dt Thus, {tex}I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{4+t^{2}-4 t} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{t^{2}-4 t+4} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} Now let us separate the integrand into the simplest form using partial fractions. {tex}\frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}}=\frac{A}{(t-2)}+\frac{B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A(t-2)+B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A t-2 A+B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex} = 3t - 2 = At - 2A + B Comparing the coefficients, we have, A = 3 and -2A + B = -2 Substituting the value of A = 3 in the above equation, we have, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 {tex} \times{/tex} 3 + B = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -6 + B = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 6 -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4 Therefore we have, {tex}I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} becomes {tex}I=\int \frac{3}{(t-2)} d t+\int \frac{4}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} = 3 log |t - 2| - 4 {tex}(\frac{1}{t-2})+C{/tex} = 3 log |2 - t| + 4 {tex}(\frac{1}{2-t})+C{/tex} Now substituting t = sin x, we have, I = 3 log |2 - sin x| +4 {tex}(\frac{1}{2-\sin x})+C{/tex}
solution: Let the given integral be I = {tex}\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\cos ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \ I=\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\left(1-\sin ^{2} x\right)-4 \sin x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 1=\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-1+\sin ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex} Substitute sin x = t => cos x dx = dt Thus, {tex}I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{4+t^{2}-4 t} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{t^{2}-4 t+4} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} Now let us separate the integrand into the simplest form using partial fractions. {tex}\frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}}=\frac{A}{(t-2)}+\frac{B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A(t-2)+B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A t-2 A+B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex} = 3t - 2 = At - 2A + B Comparing the coefficients, we have, A = 3 and -2A + B = -2 Substituting the value of A = 3 in the above equation, we have, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 {tex} \times{/tex} 3 + B = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -6 + B = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 6 -2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4 Therefore we have, {tex}I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} becomes {tex}I=\int \frac{3}{(t-2)} d t+\int \frac{4}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} = 3 log |t - 2| - 4 {tex}(\frac{1}{t-2})+C{/tex} = 3 log |2 - t| + 4 {tex}(\frac{1}{2-t})+C{/tex} Now substituting t = sin x, we have, I = 3 log |2 - sin x| +4 {tex}(\frac{1}{2-\sin x})+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let the given integral be

I = {tex}\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\cos ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ I=\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-\left(1-\sin ^{2} x\right)-4 \sin x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 1=\int \frac{(3 \sin x-2) \cos x}{5-1+\sin ^{2} x-4 \sin x} d x{/tex}
Substitute sin x = t
=> cos x dx = dt
Thus,
{tex}I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{4+t^{2}-4 t} d t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{t^{2}-4 t+4} d t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex}
Now let us separate the integrand into the simplest form using partial fractions.
{tex}\frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}}=\frac{A}{(t-2)}+\frac{B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{A(t-2)+B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{A t-2 A+B}{(t-2)^{2}}{/tex}
= 3t - 2 = At - 2A + B
Comparing the coefficients, we have,
A = 3 and -2A + B = -2
Substituting the value of A = 3 in the above equation, we have,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}-2 {tex} \times{/tex} 3 + B = -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}-6 + B = -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 6 -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4
Therefore we have, {tex}I=\int \frac{(3 t-2)}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex} becomes
{tex}I=\int \frac{3}{(t-2)} d t+\int \frac{4}{(t-2)^{2}} d t{/tex}
= 3 log |t - 2| - 4 {tex}(\frac{1}{t-2})+C{/tex}
= 3 log |2 - t| + 4 {tex}(\frac{1}{2-t})+C{/tex}
Now substituting t = sin x, we have,
I = 3 log |2 - sin x| +4 {tex}(\frac{1}{2-\sin x})+C{/tex}

solution_html:
According to the question ,  {tex} I =\int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 / 2 } | x \cos \pi x | d x{/tex}
consider, x cos {tex} \pi{/tex}x = 0
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad x = 0 \text { or } \cos \pi x = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad x = 0 \text { or } \pi x = \frac { \pi } { 2 } , \frac { 3 \pi } { 2 } , \frac { 5 \pi } { 2 }{/tex}[{tex} \because \cos \frac { n \pi } { 2 } = 0{/tex}, n being an odd integer]
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad x = 0 \text { or } x = \frac { 1 } { 2 } , \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad x = 0 , \frac { 1 } { 2 } , \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}
So, let us divide the integral by x = {tex} \frac 12{/tex} , we get 
Now ,
{tex} | x \cos \pi x | = \left\{ \begin{array} { c c } { x \cos \pi x , } & { 0 \leq x \leq \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \\ { - x \cos \pi x , } & { \frac { 1 } { 2 } \leq x \leq \frac { 3 } { 2 } } \end{array} \right.{/tex}
Now, {tex}I =\int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 / 2 } | x \cos \pi x | d x + \int _ { 1 / 2 } ^ { 3 / 2 } |x \cos \pi x | d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 / 2 } x \cos \pi x d x - \int _ { 1 / 2 } ^ { 3 / 2 } x \cos \pi x d x{/tex}..........(i)
Let I1 = {tex}\int_{} {\mathop x\limits_I } \mathop {\cos \pi x}\limits_{II} dx{/tex}
By Using integration by parts  , we get 
{tex}= x \frac { \sin \pi x } { \pi } - \int \frac { \sin \pi x } { \pi } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { x \sin \pi x } { \pi } + \frac { \cos \pi x } { \pi ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
Now, From Eq.(i), we have
{tex}I =\left[ x \frac { \sin \pi x } { \pi } + \frac { \cos \pi x } { \pi ^ { 2 } } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 / 2 } - \left[ \frac { x \sin \pi x } { \pi } + \frac { \cos \pi x } { \pi ^ { 2 } } \right] _ { 1 / 2 } ^ { 3 / 2 }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 \pi } \sin \frac { \pi } { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { \pi ^ { 2 } } \cos \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) - \left( 0 + \frac { \cos 0 } { \pi ^ { 2 } } \right) \right]{/tex}{tex}- \left[ \left( \frac { 3 } { 2 \pi } \sin \frac { 3 \pi } { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { \pi ^ { 2 } } \cos \frac { 3 \pi } { 2 } \right)\right.{/tex}{tex}- \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 \pi } \sin \frac { \pi } { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { \pi ^ { 2 } } \cos \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) ]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 \pi } - \frac { 1 } { \pi ^ { 2 } } - \left( \frac { - 3 } { 2 \pi } - \frac { 1 } { 2 \pi } \right){/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \pi } - \frac { 1 } { \pi ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 3 } { 2 \pi } + \frac { 1 } { 2 \pi }{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 5 } { 2 \pi } - \frac { 1 } { \pi ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 5 \pi - 2 } { 2 \pi ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \frac { 5 \pi - 2 } { 2 \pi ^ { 2 } }{/tex} sq units.

explanation: According to the given question , {tex} I =\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } d x{/tex} By using partial fractions , let {tex} \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 1 } + \frac { B } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { C } { x + 2 }{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = A(x + 1) (x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x + 1) 2 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = A(x 2 + 3x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x 2 + 2x + 1) {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = (A + C) x 2 + (3A + B + 2C) x + (2A + 2B + C) On comparing the coefficients of {tex}x^2 , x{/tex} and constant , we get A + C = 1 ...(ii) 3A + B + 2C = 1 ...(iii) and 2A + 2B + C = 1 ...(iv) On solving equation (ii),(iii) and (iv), we get A = -2, B = 1, and C = 3. From Eq. (i), we get {tex} \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } = \frac { - 2 } { x + 1 } + \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 3 } { x + 2 }{/tex} On Integrating both sides w.r.t to x, we get {tex} \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } d x = - 2 \int \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex} + \int \frac { d x } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + 3 \int \frac { d x } { ( x + 2 ) }{/tex} {tex} = - 2 \log | x + 1 | - \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } + 3 \log | x + 2 | + C{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = - 2 \log | x + 1 | - \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } + 3 \log | x + 2 | + C{/tex}
solution: According to the given question , {tex} I =\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } d x{/tex} By using partial fractions , let {tex} \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 1 } + \frac { B } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { C } { x + 2 }{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = A(x + 1) (x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x + 1) 2 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = A(x 2 + 3x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x 2 + 2x + 1) {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = (A + C) x 2 + (3A + B + 2C) x + (2A + 2B + C) On comparing the coefficients of {tex}x^2 , x{/tex} and constant , we get A + C = 1 ...(ii) 3A + B + 2C = 1 ...(iii) and 2A + 2B + C = 1 ...(iv) On solving equation (ii),(iii) and (iv), we get A = -2, B = 1, and C = 3. From Eq. (i), we get {tex} \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } = \frac { - 2 } { x + 1 } + \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 3 } { x + 2 }{/tex} On Integrating both sides w.r.t to x, we get {tex} \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } d x = - 2 \int \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex} + \int \frac { d x } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + 3 \int \frac { d x } { ( x + 2 ) }{/tex} {tex} = - 2 \log | x + 1 | - \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } + 3 \log | x + 2 | + C{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = - 2 \log | x + 1 | - \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } + 3 \log | x + 2 | + C{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the given question , {tex} I =\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } d x{/tex}
By using partial fractions ,
let {tex} \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 1 } + \frac { B } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { C } { x + 2 }{/tex} ...(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + x + 1 = A(x + 1) (x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x + 1)2
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + x + 1 = A(x2 + 3x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x2 + 2x + 1)
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + x + 1 = (A + C) x2 + (3A + B + 2C) x + (2A + 2B + C)
On comparing the coefficients of {tex}x^2 , x{/tex} and constant , we get
A + C = 1 ...(ii)
3A + B + 2C = 1 ...(iii)
and 2A + 2B + C = 1 ...(iv)
On solving equation (ii),(iii) and (iv), we get
A = -2,
B = 1, and
C = 3.
From Eq. (i), we get
{tex} \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } = \frac { - 2 } { x + 1 } + \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 3 } { x + 2 }{/tex}
On Integrating both sides w.r.t to x, we get 
{tex} \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } + x + 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } ( x + 2 ) } d x = - 2 \int \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex} + \int \frac { d x } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } + 3 \int \frac { d x } { ( x + 2 ) }{/tex}
{tex} = - 2 \log | x + 1 | - \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } + 3 \log | x + 2 | + C{/tex}
{tex} \therefore I = - 2 \log | x + 1 | - \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } + 3 \log | x + 2 | + C{/tex}

{tex} = \int \frac { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \log \left| \frac { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } { x ^ { 2 } } \right| } { x ^ { 4 } } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \frac { m } { n } \right]{/tex}

{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{\cos ^{2} x+4(1)-\left(4 \cos ^{2} x\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\frac{1}{3} \cdot 3 \cos ^{2} x}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{-3 \cos ^{2} x+4-4}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4-3 \cos ^{2} x}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1) d x+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4}{4-3\left(\frac{1}{\sec ^{2} x}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot[\mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4 \sec ^{2} \mathrm{x}}{4 \sec ^{2} \mathrm{x}-3} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot\left[\frac{\pi}{2}\right]+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4 \sec ^{2} x}{4\left(1+\tan ^{2} x\right)-3} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{\pi}{6}+\frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} \mathrm{x}}{1+4 \tan ^{2} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{\pi}{6}+\mathrm{I}_{1}{/tex} .....(ii) First solve for I 1 : {tex}I_{1}=\frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} x}{1+4 \tan ^{2} x} d x{/tex} Let 2 tan x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 sec 2 x dx dt When x = 0 then t = 0 and when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} then t = {tex}\infty{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} x}{1+4 \tan ^{2} x} d x=\frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{1+t^{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{2}{3}\left[\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right]_{0}^{\infty}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}\left[\tan ^{-1} \infty-\tan ^{-1} 0\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{2}{3} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} Put this value in equ.(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{\pi}{6}+\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{\cos ^{2} x+4 \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{\cos ^{2} x+4 \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} ......(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}+4\left(1-\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}\right)} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{\cos ^{2} x+4(1)-\left(4 \cos ^{2} x\right)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\frac{1}{3} \cdot 3 \cos ^{2} x}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{-3 \cos ^{2} x+4-4}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4-3 \cos ^{2} x}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4}{4-3 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1) d x+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4}{4-3\left(\frac{1}{\sec ^{2} x}\right)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot[\mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4 \sec ^{2} \mathrm{x}}{4 \sec ^{2} \mathrm{x}-3} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \cdot\left[\frac{\pi}{2}\right]+\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{4 \sec ^{2} x}{4\left(1+\tan ^{2} x\right)-3} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{\pi}{6}+\frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} \mathrm{x}}{1+4 \tan ^{2} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{\pi}{6}+\mathrm{I}_{1}{/tex} .....(ii)
First solve for I1:
{tex}I_{1}=\frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} x}{1+4 \tan ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
Let 2 tan x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 sec2x dx dt
When x = 0 then t = 0 and when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} then t = {tex}\infty{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} x}{1+4 \tan ^{2} x} d x=\frac{2}{3} \cdot \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{1+t^{2}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{2}{3}\left[\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right]_{0}^{\infty}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{3}\left[\tan ^{-1} \infty-\tan ^{-1} 0\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{2}{3} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} 
Put this value in equ.(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{\pi}{6}+\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}

\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)} d x{/tex} By using property of definite integrals, {tex} \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x{/tex} , we get {tex} I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\frac{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}+\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} 2} \frac{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}+2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1} d x{/tex} {tex}\text { Let } \quad \tan \frac{x}{2}=t . \text { Then, } d\left(\tan \frac{x}{2}\right)={/tex} {tex}d t t}{-\left[(t-1)^{2}-2\right]}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{(\sqrt{2})^{2}-(t-1)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\pi \times\left.\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log | \sqrt{2}} \log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}}{(\sqrt{2}-1)(\sqrt{2}+1)}\right\}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}=\frac{\pi}{\sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex}

\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)} d x{/tex} By using property of definite integrals, {tex} \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x{/tex} , we get {tex} I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} ...(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x+\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\frac{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}+\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}+2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1} d x{/tex} {tex}\text { Let } \quad \tan \frac{x}{2}=t . \text { Then, } d\left(\tan \frac{x}{2}\right)={/tex} {tex}d t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} d x=d t \Rightarrow d x=\frac{2 d t}{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{/tex} Also, {tex}x=0 \Rightarrow t=\tan 0=0 \text { and } x=\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow t=\tan \frac{\pi}{4}=1{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 d t}{-t^{2}+2 t+1}={/tex} {tex}\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{-\left(t^{2}-2 t-1\right)}=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{-\left[(t-1)^{2}-2\right]}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{(\sqrt{2})^{2}-(t-1)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\pi \times\left.\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log | \frac{\sqrt{2}+t-1}{\sqrt{2}-t+1}\right]\right|_{0} ^{1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log 1-\log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}}{(\sqrt{2}-1)(\sqrt{2}+1)}\right\}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}=\frac{\pi}{\sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {tex}\text { Let } I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)} d x{/tex}
By using property of definite integrals,{tex} \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x{/tex}, we get
{tex} I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{x+\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\frac{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}+\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1+\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{-\tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}+2 \tan \frac{x}{2}+1} d x{/tex}
{tex}\text { Let } \quad \tan \frac{x}{2}=t . \text { Then, } d\left(\tan \frac{x}{2}\right)={/tex}{tex}d t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} d x=d t \Rightarrow d x=\frac{2 d t}{\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{/tex}
Also, {tex}x=0 \Rightarrow t=\tan 0=0 \text { and } x=\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow t=\tan \frac{\pi}{4}=1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 d t}{-t^{2}+2 t+1}={/tex}{tex}\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{-\left(t^{2}-2 t-1\right)}=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{-\left[(t-1)^{2}-2\right]}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{(\sqrt{2})^{2}-(t-1)^{2}}{/tex}{tex}=\pi \times\left.\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log | \frac{\sqrt{2}+t-1}{\sqrt{2}-t+1}\right]\right|_{0} ^{1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\log 1-\log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)\right]{/tex}{tex}=-\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}}{(\sqrt{2}-1)(\sqrt{2}+1)}\right\}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1)^{2}=\frac{\pi}{\sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}} \log (\sqrt{2}+1){/tex}

solution: I = {tex}\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^3}x.\sin (x + \alpha )} }}} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^4}x.\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }} = \int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}dx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} } {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}xdx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin x.\cos \alpha + \cos x.\sin \alpha }}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{\cos e{c^2}2dx}}{{\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha } }}} {/tex} Put {tex}\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha = t{/tex} {tex}0 - \ cose{c^2}x.\sin \alpha dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\ cose{c^2}xdx = \frac{{ - dt}}{{\sin \alpha }}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex} I= \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}} \times \frac{{{t^{1/2}}}}{{1/2}} + c{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt t }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x\sin \alpha } }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^3}x.\sin (x + \alpha )} }}} {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^4}x.\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }} = \int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}dx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} } {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}xdx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin x.\cos \alpha + \cos x.\sin \alpha }}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex}
{tex}=\int {\frac{{\cos e{c^2}2dx}}{{\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha } }}} {/tex}

Put {tex}\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha = t{/tex}

{tex}0 - \ cose{c^2}x.\sin \alpha dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\ cose{c^2}xdx = \frac{{ - dt}}{{\sin \alpha }}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex} I= \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}} \times \frac{{{t^{1/2}}}}{{1/2}} + c{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt t }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x\sin \alpha } }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex}

(i) and (ii), we get {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi \tan x } { \sec x + \tan x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \tan x } { \sec x + \tan x } d x {/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \frac {\ sinx}{cosx} } { \frac {1}{cosx} + \frac {\ sinx }{cosx}} d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \sin x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \sin x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 + \sin x - 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \frac { 1 + \sin x } { 1 + \sin x } - \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( 1 - \sec x ) + 2 ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) \right\}{/tex} {tex}2 I = \pi \{ \pi - 0 + 2 ( 0 - 1 ) \} = \pi ( \pi - 2 ){/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { \pi } { 2 } ( \pi - 2 ){/tex}
solution_html:
According to given question ,{tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x \tan x } { \sec x + \tan x } d x{/tex} ...(i)
we know that , 
{tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) \tan ( \pi - x ) } { \sec ( \pi - x ) + \tan ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { - ( \pi - x ) \tan x } { - \sec x - \tan x } d x{/tex} {tex}[\because tan(\pi-x) = -tanx , sec (\pi-x) = -secx ]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) \tan x } { \sec x + \tan x } d x{/tex} ...(ii)
On adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi \tan x } { \sec x + \tan x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \tan x } { \sec x + \tan x } d x {/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \frac {\ sinx}{cosx} } { \frac {1}{cosx} + \frac {\ sinx }{cosx}} d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \sin x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \sin x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 + \sin x - 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \frac { 1 + \sin x } { 1 + \sin x } - \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( 1 - \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } 1 d x - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } 1 d x - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x \right]{/tex}{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 a } f ( x ) = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x } \ \ { \text { if } \ \ f ( 2 a - x ) = f ( x ) } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } d x - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ [ x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x \right]{/tex}{tex}\left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x = 1 \Rightarrow 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x = \cos ^ { 2 } x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ [ x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ ( \pi - 0 ) - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \sec x \tan x \right) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left\{ \pi - 2 [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \right\}{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \left\{ \pi - \lim _ { x \rightarrow \frac { \pi } { 2 } } 2 ( \tan x - \sec x ) + 2 ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) \right\}{/tex}
{tex}2 I = \pi \{ \pi - 0 + 2 ( 0 - 1 ) \} = \pi ( \pi - 2 ){/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { \pi } { 2 } ( \pi - 2 ){/tex}

question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} {/tex}
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} {/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} dx{/tex} …(i) {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{{a }{}} {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)} dx } } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\sin }^5x}}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}dx} {/tex} …(ii) Adding equations (i) and (ii), {tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}} + \frac{{{{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} dx{/tex} …(i) {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{{a }{}} {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)} dx } } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\sin }^5x}}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}dx} {/tex} …(ii) Adding equations (i) and (ii), {tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}} + \frac{{{{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} dx{/tex}…(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{{a }{}} {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)} dx } } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\sin }^5x}}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}dx} {/tex} …(ii)
Adding equations (i) and (ii),
{tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}} + \frac{{{{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

question_html: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex}
question_text: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x + 2} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{A}{{x + 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 2}} + \frac{C}{{x - 2}}{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1)(x - 2) + C(x + 1)(x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x 2 4) + B(x 2 - x - 2) + C(x 2 + 3x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow 2x {/tex} = Ax 2 - 4A + Bx 2 - Bx - 2B + Cx 2 + 3Cx + 2C Comparing coefficients of x 2 : A + B + C = 0 .......(ii) Comparing coefficients of x: B + 3C= 5 .......(iii) Comparing constants: 4A 2B + 2C = 0 .......(iv) On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{5}{3},B = \frac{{ - 5}}{2},C = \frac{5}{6}{/tex} Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i), {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\frac{5}{3}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}}}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{6}}}{{x - 2}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{5}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}}dx + \frac{5}{6}} \int {\frac{1}{{x - 2}}} } dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{3}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 2} \right| + \frac{5}{6}\log \left| {x - 2} \right| + c{/tex}
solution: {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x + 2} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{A}{{x + 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 2}} + \frac{C}{{x - 2}}{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1)(x - 2) + C(x + 1)(x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x 2 4) + B(x 2 - x - 2) + C(x 2 + 3x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow 2x {/tex} = Ax 2 - 4A + Bx 2 - Bx - 2B + Cx 2 + 3Cx + 2C Comparing coefficients of x 2 : A + B + C = 0 .......(ii) Comparing coefficients of x: B + 3C= 5 .......(iii) Comparing constants: 4A 2B + 2C = 0 .......(iv) On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{5}{3},B = \frac{{ - 5}}{2},C = \frac{5}{6}{/tex} Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i), {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\frac{5}{3}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}}}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{6}}}{{x - 2}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{5}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}}dx + \frac{5}{6}} \int {\frac{1}{{x - 2}}} } dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{3}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 2} \right| + \frac{5}{6}\log \left| {x - 2} \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x + 2} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{A}{{x + 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 2}} + \frac{C}{{x - 2}}{/tex} ...(i)

{tex} \Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1)(x - 2) + C(x + 1)(x + 2)

{tex}\Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x2 4) + B(x2 - x - 2) + C(x2 + 3x + 2)

{tex}\Rightarrow 2x {/tex} = Ax2 - 4A + Bx2 - Bx - 2B + Cx2 + 3Cx + 2C

Comparing coefficients of x2: A + B + C = 0 .......(ii)
Comparing coefficients of x: B + 3C= 5 .......(iii)
Comparing constants: 4A 2B + 2C = 0 .......(iv)
On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{5}{3},B = \frac{{ - 5}}{2},C = \frac{5}{6}{/tex}
Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i),

{tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\frac{5}{3}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}}}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{6}}}{{x - 2}}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \int \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}dx{/tex}{tex}= \frac{5}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{5}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}}dx + \frac{5}{6}} \int {\frac{1}{{x - 2}}} } dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{5}{3}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 2} \right| + \frac{5}{6}\log \left| {x - 2} \right| + c{/tex}
